name: Axarquía Coast and Inland Loop
name_original: La Axarquía – circular por la costa e interior desde Almáchar
description: This difficult 29.6-mile mountain biking route from Almáchar explores La Axarquía's diverse coast and interior, gaining 2144 feet.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Almáchar offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, agricultural valleys, and views extending towards the Axarquía coast. The region features a mix of challenging climbs and varied terrain, including both inland and coastal paths. Elevation gains can be significant, providing demanding routes for experienced mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails in Almáchar The most popular mountain bike trail is Axarquía Coast and Inland Loop, a 29.6 miles (47.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route combines coastal views with inland exploration. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Almáchar – Raisin Day in El Borge loop from Almáchar, a difficult 17.1 miles (27.5 km) path. This route winds through the local agricultural landscape, known for its raisin production. Local mountain bikers also love the Fountain loop from Huerta de los Ranea, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Almáchar is defined by its hilly inland terrain, agricultural areas, and occasional coastal vistas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to challenging mountain bike trails. The routes in Almáchar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 100 reviews. Nearly 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Almáchar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Almáchar?
A: There are over 90 mountain bike trails around Almáchar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from moderate routes to very challenging climbs.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Almáchar?
A: Yes, Almáchar offers several easier mountain bike trails. Approximately 10 routes are classified as easy, providing a good starting point for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride through the agricultural valleys.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails in the Almáchar area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Almáchar has over 40 difficult mountain bike trails. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994160079/axarquia-coast-and-inland-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Axarquía Coast and Inland Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 48 km with significant elevation, offer demanding climbs and varied terrain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17245583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almáchar – Raisin Day in El Borge loop from Almáchar&lt;/a&gt;, known for its steep ascents through the local agricultural landscape.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Almáchar?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Almáchar are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38275765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain loop from Huerta de los Ranea&lt;/a&gt;, a 41 km route that takes you through varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Almáchar?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Almáchar is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable rides through the region's hilly inland terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with good viewpoints in Almáchar?
A: Yes, the region around Almáchar offers trails with excellent viewpoints, often extending towards the Axarquía coast. Many routes traverse rolling hills and elevated areas, providing panoramic vistas. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/406395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del León Descent&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3373221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Tío Cañas&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into longer rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Almáchar?
A: Mountain biking around Almáchar is characterized by hilly inland terrain, agricultural areas, and occasional coastal vistas. You can expect a mix of challenging climbs and varied surfaces, including both dirt tracks and paved sections, reflecting the region's diverse landscape.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see along the trails?
A: Beyond the scenic trails, you can explore several natural and historical landmarks. The area features various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/684887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolina Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/862950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Sol (1085 m)&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3007295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Salazar Geodesic Vertex&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Lagar de Omitale on Cerro Olmo&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's geography and history.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Almáchar?
A: The mountain bike trails in Almáchar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscape, the challenging climbs, and the rewarding views that combine inland exploration with coastal glimpses.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes, for example, around 20km or more?
A: Yes, Almáchar offers numerous longer mountain bike routes. Many trails exceed 20km, with some, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38279621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain – El Saltillo Suspension Bridge loop from Cútar&lt;/a&gt;, extending to over 80km. These longer routes provide ample opportunity for extended rides and exploration of the wider Axarquía region.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails in Almáchar?
A: Yes, Almáchar has a significant number of moderate mountain bike trails, with over 40 routes falling into this category. These trails offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility, suitable for riders with some experience. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19347401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benamargosa – Fountain loop from Cútar&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 38.7 km route.
tips: A beautiful and flowery town in La Axarquía that lived through some bloody events at the beginning of the 20th century. The town rebelled against a possible electoral fraud that resulted in many injuries and arrests, as well as the death of a civil guard whose head rolled throughout the town and ended up in the river, never being found and turning the story into legend.
You can do as many rounds as you like in these hills, short and crisp. Best suited if you don't have a lot of time.

This car park at the old train station in Torre de Benagalbón is a great starting point for a variety of different tours; Mountain biking or hiking in the mountains, a leisurely bike ride and the whole family along the beach or just a walk. Countless small beach bars invite you for a beer, and there is also excellent fish here. The parking lot can get very crowded in the summer months!

Typical white town of Málaga of about 1600 inhabitants in which its parish church dedicated to San Mateo stands out. This was built in the 16th century and combines late Gothic elements with some Renaissance and Mudejar details.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This challenging 47.6-kilometer circular route is estimated to take approximately 4 hours and 2 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level, riding speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as 'difficult' and is best suited for experienced mountain bikers with good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. It features significant elevation changes and technical sections, corresponding to an STS S3-S6 technical difficulty rating. Beginners may find it too demanding.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers a highly varied landscape, transitioning between mountains, valleys, and the Mediterranean coastline. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces including unpaved sections (15.6 km), gravel (13.7 km), paved parts (8.35 km), natural trails (4.79 km), and asphalt (4.13 km). Be prepared for diverse conditions, including some sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Almáchar. While specific parking information isn't provided, Almáchar, being a village, typically offers public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability upon arrival in the village center.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural insights. Key highlights include passing through the charming Almáchar village, experiencing the Benagalbón Village Center, and riding along sections like the Barrio Cortijo Blanco Trail and the Paseo de la Marina, which offers coastal views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The La Axarquía region generally enjoys a mild climate. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) are often considered the best times, offering pleasant temperatures and less extreme weather for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the La Axarquía – circular por la costa e interior desde Almáchar trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions against dogs on this specific trail, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in agricultural areas, near livestock, or on shared paths. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a challenging 47.6 km ride with significant elevation changes.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route in La Axarquía?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this mountain bike route in La Axarquía. The trail primarily uses public paths and roads. However, always respect private property and local regulations, especially when passing through villages or agricultural land.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it includes parts of the Rincón de la Victoria - Vélez-Málaga trail (30% of the route) and La Senda Litoral (20% of the route). You'll also briefly cross sections of the Almánchar - Cerro Patarra trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate protective gear. A GPS device with the route loaded is highly recommended, as well as sun protection and layers of clothing suitable for changing conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities available along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Almáchar, where you can find cafes and other amenities. As the trail traverses both coastal and interior areas, you may pass through other small villages or towns that offer opportunities for refreshments, but it's wise to carry sufficient supplies as services might be sparse in some rural sections.
